items.title: v0.4 Phase 27 — Floor Plan Extraction
items.desc: Context-aware vision extraction of floor plans, P&IDs, and details. Anti-hallucination prompts inject schedule-built equipment checklist. Plan 27-01 (infrastructure) complete, 27-02 (Refrigeration P&IDs) next, 27-03 (remaining MEP disciplines).

items.title: Procore Drawing Zip Import
items.desc: Extract PDFs from Procore zip exports and route to project discipline folders. Handle revision superseding.

items.title: Batch WPQ/WPS Extraction
items.desc: Extract ~500 welding PDFs from D:\RAW_DATA\ using dual Sonnet + Opus shadow review pipeline.

items.title: Drawing Viewer (pdf.js)
items.desc: Side-by-side PDF viewer with extracted data overlay. pdf.js renders drawing pages in browser, equipment tags link to page/location, click-to-jump from equipment detail or conflict. Split-pane layout: drawing on left, extracted data/conflicts on right. Per-sheet review status tracking. Critical for making the system a usable review tool rather than just a database.

items.title: Conflict Resolution UI
items.desc: Make conflicts actionable: resolve (with note), mark false positive, assign to reviewer, generate RFI draft. Bulk actions for batch resolution. Resolution audit trail. Filter by status (new/confirmed/assigned/resolved/false_positive). Currently conflicts are view-only on dashboard — this adds the workflow to close them out.

items.title: Remaining Schedule Extraction (40 sheets)
items.desc: 19 of 59 Vital schedule sheets extracted. 40 remaining include architectural, civil, structural, fire protection, and additional MEP sheets. Would increase equipment registry coverage and improve conflict detection accuracy.

items.title: Legend Extraction (11 sheets)
items.desc: Extract P&ID legends, abbreviation tables, and symbol keys from 11 legend/notes sheets. Store in drawing_abbreviations table for authoritative instrument/component classification. Resolves naming inconsistencies (Control Valve vs Electronic Expansion Valve). Was originally planned as Phase 26 before schedule reconciliation took priority.

items.title: Specification Intake & Requirements Extraction
items.desc: Parse project specification PDFs by CSI division. Extract: approved manufacturers, material requirements, test/inspection requirements, submittal requirements per section. Link spec sections to equipment types for automated compliance checking. Build on existing sis-spec-intake foundation.

items.title: Submittal Builder (PM/Vendor Tool)
items.desc: Web-based submittal creation tool for PMs and vendors. Pre-populates from schedule_extractions: equipment tags, spec sections, required manufacturers, quantities. Wizard flow: (1) Select project + spec section, (2) System shows required equipment from drawings, (3) Vendor uploads product data sheets/cut sheets per item, (4) System validates completeness (all required items covered, spec section matches), (5) Generates formatted submittal package with standardized naming (project-spec_section-revision), cover sheet, table of contents, and item cross-reference matrix. Enforces consistent structure so downstream submittal intake/review is predictable. Supports resubmittal workflow (track revisions, reviewer comments, approval status). Vendor portal option for external access without full QMS login.

items.title: Submittal Intake & Cross-Reference
items.desc: AI-powered submittal processing: extract manufacturer, model, spec section, approved/rejected status from submittal PDFs. Cross-reference against schedule_extractions to verify submitted equipment matches design specs. Flag mismatches (wrong model, missing submittals, unapproved substitutions). Schema: submittals table + submittal_items with FK to equipment. Builder-generated submittals get fast-tracked (structure already known); external submittals get full AI extraction.

items.title: Quality Documentation Package Generator & Tracker
items.desc: Quality department deliverable to operations: generates pre-filled test and inspection documents for every piece of equipment on the project, organized by system and project stage. Philosophy: QA provides the field with ready-to-execute packages where all the hard parts (equipment tags, spec references, acceptance criteria, design values, related drawing numbers) are already filled in — the field only needs to record actual test results and sign. This removes the documentation burden from operations while ensuring QA gets complete, high-quality data back. Three phases: (1) GENERATE — Cross-reference quality manual M3 programs, project specifications (test/inspection requirements per CSI section), and equipment registry to produce a master documentation matrix. For each equipment tag or system, determine every required document (hydrostatic test, insulation resistance, weld inspection, pressure test, balancing report, startup checklist, etc.) and at which project stage it's needed (pre-install, install, startup, commissioning, closeout). Generate pre-filled PDF/form packages per system with equipment-specific data populated from the drawing database (tag, location, design pressure, design temp, voltage, HP, flow rates, acceptance criteria from specs). (2) TRACK — Web dashboard showing overall project quality documentation status. System-level drill-down: 'Chilled Water System: 14 of 18 documents complete'. Equipment-level: 'CH-1: startup report pending, hydro test complete'. Stage gates: cannot mark system commissioning-ready until all install-stage docs are complete. Supports self-perform QC (SIS crews) and subcontractor document collection with the same pre-filled templates. (3) CLOSE — Completed documents are processed back into the project quality database. System assembles final turnover packages per system for owner delivery: all test reports, inspection records, certifications, calibration certs, O&M manuals, and as-built data in structured format. Turnover package generation is automated — when all documents for a system are marked complete, the package is ready to print/export.

items.title: Export & Reports
items.desc: Downloadable deliverables from the equipment registry: conflict report PDF (grouped by severity, with drawing references), equipment register CSV/Excel export, system summary report, discipline coverage matrix. WeasyPrint for PDFs, openpyxl for Excel. Needed for coordination meetings and owner deliverables.

items.title: Multi-Project Onboarding
items.desc: Streamlined workflow to add a new project to the equipment platform: import drawings, run schedule extraction, reconcile, detect conflicts. Currently only Vital (07645) is onboarded. Need to prove the system works across projects. Includes project template, discipline auto-detection, extraction queue management.

items.title: RFI Intake & Tracking
items.desc: Process RFI PDFs: extract question, response, referenced drawings/specs, status, dates, responsible parties. Link RFIs to affected equipment tags and drawing sheets. Track open/closed status, response time metrics. Web UI for RFI log with filters and search.

items.title: Quality Manual Module 3 XML Authoring
items.desc: Author module3_output.xml from planning doc. 8 sections: Program Framework (3.1), Welding Quality (3.2), Training & Workforce (3.3), Testing & Calibration (3.4), CI/CAPA (3.5), Field Operations Manual (3.6), Vendor & Subcontractor Quality (3.7), Internal Audit (3.8).

items.title: Quality Manual Module 4 XML Authoring
items.desc: Author module4_output.xml from planning doc. ~50 SOPs across 11 categories with dual ID system (section 4.X + SOP-XXX-NNN). Reserved section ranges per category.

items.title: Windows Service (nssm)
items.desc: Run Waitress as auto-start Windows service instead of manual qms serve.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 27-01 — Floor Plan Extraction Infrastructure
items.desc: floor_plan_extractions table (15 columns, appearance_type tracking), floor_plan_extractor.py with context-aware vision prompts (anti-hallucination checklist from schedules), harness phase='plans' support. 344 pending sheets for Vital.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 26 — Schedule Reconciliation
items.desc: Integrated 452 schedule extraction entries as 12th reconciler data source. 661 instances (was 465), 296 enriched with manufacturer/model/HP/voltage/weight from schedules, conflicts reduced 496→473. enrich_from_schedules() with full attribute_log audit trail.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 25 — Schedule-First Extraction (3 plans)
items.desc: Complete Vital MEP schedule extraction: Docling structural + 19 Sonnet vision agents + Opus shadow QA. 452 equipment entries across 19 sheets, 5 disciplines (Electrical=207, Mechanical=89, Utility=88, Refrigeration=43, Plumbing=25). Shadow review caught M6001 75% Docling gap. Source tracking + tiered confidence on all entries.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 24 — System Model & Consolidation
items.desc: System type taxonomy (15 types), equipment_system_members junction table, system builder consolidating 13 RS-* circuits into 5 real refrigeration systems, 5 plumbing systems, 3 mechanical systems. System assignment 23% to 82.8%. Dashboard System|Grouped|All three-mode toggle.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 23 — Equipment Hierarchy & Tag Dedup
items.desc: Tag parser for parent-child hierarchy, reversed tag normalization, dedup 124 duplicate pairs (589→465), sub-component type reclassification (CV→Control Valve, etc.), grouped dashboard view toggle (Grouped|All) with expandable parent rows.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 22 — Equipment Web UI
items.desc: Equipment dashboard with project selector, 6 stats cards, conflict summary, filterable equipment table. Detail page with tabbed view (Overview, Connections, Conflicts). Blueprint with 2 page routes + 4 JSON API endpoints.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 21 — Spec Compliance & Impact Chains
items.desc: Spec compliance engine with 9 configurable requirements (6 check types), equipment impact chain analyzer via BFS graph traversal (524 relationships), drawing revision impact assessment, violation propagation tracing. 4 spec violations detected for Vital.

items.title: v0.4 Phase 20 — Conflict Detection & Negative Space
items.desc: Cross-discipline attribute conflict detection (rule-based with tolerance), missing-discipline negative space scanner, CLI command with summary and RFI output. 492 conflicts detected for Vital (2 attribute, 490 missing discipline).

items.title: v0.4 Phase 19 — Equipment Registry Schema & Reconciliation
items.desc: 10-table equipment registry with Type/Variant/Instance model, reconciliation engine scanning 11 extraction tables, system groupings, relationship graph. Vital populated with 585 instances, 23 systems, 524 relationships.

items.title: v0.3 Phase 14 — Schema & API Foundation (2 plans)
items.desc: M3 programs + M4 SOPs schema (7 tables), qualitydocs/db.py CRUD module with 22 functions, 15 seeded M4 categories, 12 REST API endpoints for programs/categories/SOPs/lifecycle/search.

items.title: v0.2 License Compliance Platform — MILESTONE COMPLETE
items.desc: 8 phases, 18 plans over 5 days. Harbor-like multi-state license compliance: foundation hardening, renewal workflows, notifications, document management, entity registration, regulatory intelligence, CE catalog, integrations/automation. Git tag: v0.2.0.
